# Ai Source Map

Converted from `30_ai_source_map.html` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

```html
<!doctype html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<title>Generative AI Source Map</title>
<style>
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif; margin: 32px; line-height: 1.45; }
table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 14px; }
th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 8px; vertical-align: top; }
th { background: #f3f3f3; text-align: left; }
tr:nth-child(even) { background: #fafafa; }
.badge { display:inline-block; padding:2px 6px; border:1px solid #999; border-radius:4px; font-size:12px; }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<h1>Generative AI Source Map</h1>
<p>This map adds current academic, regulator, platform, security, and buyer-behavior sources to the original study pack. Vendor and platform sources are useful for current market direction, but they are not neutral. Treat them as signals, not laws.</p>
<table>
<thead><tr><th>Priority</th><th>Category</th><th>Source</th><th>Authors/Org</th><th>Year</th><th>Bias / caveat</th><th>Why study it</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>Generative AI in marketing</td>
      <td><a href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01064-3" target="_blank">How generative AI Is shaping the future of marketing</a></td>
      <td>Dhruv Grewal; Cinthia B. Satornino; Thomas Davenport; Abhijit Guha</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Academic; broad strategy lens; not a tactical implementation manual</td>
      <td>High-level map of how GenAI changes marketing research, strategy, actions, customer experience, and firm processes.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>Generative AI in marketing</td>
      <td><a href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11747-024-01044-7" target="_blank">Generative AI in innovation and marketing processes</a></td>
      <td>Paola Cillo; Gaia Rubera</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Academic; opportunity framing</td>
      <td>Useful for seeing GenAI as part of product, innovation, and marketing processes rather than copy generation alone.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>Generative AI in advertising practice</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2025.2604910" target="_blank">Transforming advertising in the age of generative AI</a></td>
      <td>J. Yang</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Qualitative/exploratory; not causal performance evidence</td>
      <td>Shows how advertising practitioners integrate GenAI into creative and production workflows.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI content marketing</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.reutterer.com/papers/reisenbichler%26reutterer%26schweidel%26dan_2022.pdf" target="_blank">Frontiers: Supporting Content Marketing with Natural Language Generation</a></td>
      <td>Martin Reisenbichler; Thomas Reutterer; David A. Schweidel; Daniel Dan</td>
      <td>2022</td>
      <td>Academic; pre-ChatGPT NLG context but still methodologically useful</td>
      <td>Grounds AI content use in semi-automated content marketing and SEO rather than vague productivity claims.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI market research</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.hbs.edu/ris/Publication%20Files/23-062_6bfe7a5b-3ed9-4afd-a4c1-c91b60dd82e5.pdf" target="_blank">Using LLMs for Market Research</a></td>
      <td>James Brand; Ayelet Israeli; Donald Ngwe</td>
      <td>2023</td>
      <td>Academic working paper; methods still developing</td>
      <td>Shows how LLMs may be used to simulate or augment market research, while requiring careful validation against real human data.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI market research</td>
      <td><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19363" target="_blank">Large Language Models for Market Research: A Data Augmentation Approach</a></td>
      <td>M. Wang et al.</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Preprint; not final peer-reviewed; technical-statistical focus</td>
      <td>Important warning: LLM-generated responses can help, but must be debiased/anchored with real human data.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI search / GEO</td>
      <td><a href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.09735" target="_blank">GEO: Generative Engine Optimization</a></td>
      <td>P. Aggarwal et al.</td>
      <td>2023/2024</td>
      <td>Early research; engines and products evolve quickly</td>
      <td>Introduces the idea that visibility in generative answer engines requires different tactics from classic SEO.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI search / GEO</td>
      <td><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.08919" target="_blank">Generative Engine Optimization: How to Dominate AI Search</a></td>
      <td>Mahe Chen; Xiaoxuan Wang; Kaiwen Chen; Nick Koudas</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Preprint; commercial relevance high but findings may shift as engines update</td>
      <td>Compares AI search and traditional search; emphasizes earned media, source authority, freshness, and machine-readable justification.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI governance and risk</td>
      <td><a href="https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf" target="_blank">Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile</a></td>
      <td>NIST</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Government/standards perspective; not marketing-specific</td>
      <td>Core risk-management source for GenAI use: validity, privacy, security, transparency, and governance.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI governance and risk</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework" target="_blank">NIST AI Risk Management Framework</a></td>
      <td>NIST</td>
      <td>2023/2024</td>
      <td>Government/standards perspective</td>
      <td>Use as your baseline for trustworthy AI management across design, deployment, monitoring, and governance.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI security</td>
      <td><a href="https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/" target="_blank">OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications</a></td>
      <td>OWASP GenAI Security Project</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Security-focused; conservative by design</td>
      <td>Needed if building AI chatbots, sales agents, support agents, website agents, or internal marketing automations.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI market context</td>
      <td><a href="https://hai.stanford.edu/ai-index/2025-ai-index-report" target="_blank">The 2025 AI Index Report</a></td>
      <td>Stanford HAI</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Academic/institutional; broad AI scope, not marketing-specific</td>
      <td>Current macro baseline for AI adoption, investment, capabilities, policy, and public perception.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI adoption / business</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai" target="_blank">The State of AI: Global Survey 2025</a></td>
      <td>McKinsey &amp; Company / QuantumBlack</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Consultancy; useful signal but incentive to frame AI transformation opportunities</td>
      <td>Current business adoption signal; useful for seeing which functions use AI and how firms report value.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI adoption / business</td>
      <td><a href="https://mlq.ai/media/quarterly_decks/v0.1_State_of_AI_in_Business_2025_Report.pdf" target="_blank">The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025</a></td>
      <td>MIT Project NANDA</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Preliminary findings; methodology should be read carefully</td>
      <td>Counterweight to hype: argues many GenAI projects fail because they are not integrated into workflows.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>AI advertising law and ethics</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes" target="_blank">FTC Announces Crackdown on Deceptive AI Claims and Schemes</a></td>
      <td>Federal Trade Commission</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Regulator perspective; enforcement-focused</td>
      <td>Essential warning against overclaiming AI capabilities, earnings outcomes, fake reviews, and AI-washing.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI advertising law and ethics</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence" target="_blank">FTC Artificial Intelligence Industry Page</a></td>
      <td>Federal Trade Commission</td>
      <td>Current</td>
      <td>Regulator perspective</td>
      <td>Use to monitor AI enforcement trends around deception, data use, endorsements, and consumer harm.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Essential</td>
      <td>Advertising law and ethics</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/topics/truth-advertising" target="_blank">Truth in Advertising</a></td>
      <td>Federal Trade Commission</td>
      <td>Current</td>
      <td>Regulator perspective</td>
      <td>Base rule: advertising claims must be truthful, not misleading, and substantiated where appropriate.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI governance and risk</td>
      <td><a href="https://oecd.ai/en/ai-principles" target="_blank">OECD AI Principles</a></td>
      <td>OECD</td>
      <td>2019/2024 update</td>
      <td>Policy lens; high-level</td>
      <td>Good global baseline for trustworthy AI: human rights, transparency, robustness, accountability.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI regulation</td>
      <td><a href="https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai" target="_blank">EU AI Act regulatory framework</a></td>
      <td>European Commission</td>
      <td>2024-2026</td>
      <td>EU regulatory perspective</td>
      <td>Required if marketing or AI products reach EU users; useful baseline for risk-based AI governance.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful/current</td>
      <td>AI regulation</td>
      <td><a href="https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/contents-code-gpai" target="_blank">General-Purpose AI Code of Practice</a></td>
      <td>European Commission</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>EU regulatory perspective; provider-focused</td>
      <td>Useful for understanding transparency, copyright, and safety expectations around general-purpose AI models.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI search / ads</td>
      <td><a href="https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16297775?hl=en" target="_blank">About ads and AI Overviews</a></td>
      <td>Google Ads Help</td>
      <td>Current</td>
      <td>Platform-owned; describes Google’s product framing</td>
      <td>Source for how ads are integrated into AI Overviews and how AI search changes ad surfaces.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful/current</td>
      <td>AI search / ads</td>
      <td><a href="https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-marketing-live-2025/" target="_blank">Google Marketing Live 2025: News and Updates</a></td>
      <td>Google</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Platform marketing announcement; product-promotion bias</td>
      <td>Tracks current AI ad products, AI Mode/AI Overviews advertising, and campaign automation direction.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>Agentic commerce</td>
      <td><a href="https://openai.com/index/buy-it-in-chatgpt/" target="_blank">Buy it in ChatGPT: Instant Checkout and the Agentic Commerce Protocol</a></td>
      <td>OpenAI</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Platform-owned; product-promotion bias</td>
      <td>Signals the movement from AI-assisted discovery to AI-mediated checkout and agentic commerce.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High/current</td>
      <td>Agentic commerce</td>
      <td><a href="https://openai.com/index/powering-product-discovery-in-chatgpt/" target="_blank">Powering Product Discovery in ChatGPT</a></td>
      <td>OpenAI</td>
      <td>2026</td>
      <td>Platform-owned; product-promotion bias</td>
      <td>Current signal for AI-native product discovery, comparison, and shopping behavior.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>Agentic commerce</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-agentic-commerce-opportunity-how-ai-agents-are-ushering-in-a-new-era-for-consumers-and-merchants" target="_blank">The agentic commerce opportunity: How AI agents are ushering in a new era for consumers and merchants</a></td>
      <td>McKinsey &amp; Company</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Consultancy; opportunity framing</td>
      <td>Useful for thinking beyond ads: buyer agents, merchant data, checkout, product feeds, and trust.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High/current</td>
      <td>B2B software buying / AI</td>
      <td><a href="https://images.g2crowd.com/uploads/attachment/file/1470753/2025-G2-Buyer-Behavior-Report.pdf" target="_blank">2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report</a></td>
      <td>G2</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Review platform; commercially biased toward review/research channels</td>
      <td>Useful current signal that AI search and review sites increasingly shape B2B software evaluation.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High/current</td>
      <td>B2B software buying / AI</td>
      <td><a href="https://go.trustradius.com/rs/827-FOI-687/images/TrustRadius-2025-Buyer-Research-Report-Top-10-Takeaways.pdf" target="_blank">B2B Tech Buying in the Age of AI</a></td>
      <td>TrustRadius</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Review platform; commercially biased, but useful buyer-behavior signal</td>
      <td>Highlights AI Overviews, fact-checking, prior experience, pricing transparency, and the trust gap in B2B tech buying.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High/current</td>
      <td>B2B software buying / AI</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-03-09-gartner-sales-survey-finds-67-percent-of-b2b-buyers-prefer-a-rep-free-experience" target="_blank">Gartner Sales Survey Finds 67% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Experience</a></td>
      <td>Gartner</td>
      <td>2026</td>
      <td>Analyst firm; press release is summarized, not full methodology</td>
      <td>Reinforces self-serve/software-buyer trend and the need to build AI-assisted buyer support instead of relying only on reps.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful/current</td>
      <td>Generative AI advertising practice</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.iab.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/IAB_GenerativeAIPlaybook_January_26.pdf" target="_blank">Generative AI Playbook for Advertising</a></td>
      <td>IAB</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Industry association; practice-heavy, less neutral than academic sources</td>
      <td>Operational view of GenAI use cases, campaign optimization, production, creative, and governance.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI creativity</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.adn5290" target="_blank">Generative AI enhances individual creativity but reduces the collective diversity of novel content</a></td>
      <td>Anil R. Doshi; Oliver P. Hauser</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Specific creative-writing context; generalize carefully</td>
      <td>Important caution: AI can improve average output but flatten diversity. This matters for ads and brand differentiation.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI disclosure / trust</td>
      <td><a href="https://ideas.repec.org/a/eee/jbrese/v186y2025ics0148296324004880.html" target="_blank">The AI-authorship effect: Understanding authenticity, moral disgust, and consumer responses to AI-generated marketing communications</a></td>
      <td>C. P. Kirk; J. Givi</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Experimental context; emotional communications may not generalize to all software ads</td>
      <td>Shows that AI authorship can reduce authenticity, positive word of mouth, and loyalty for some emotional communications.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI disclosure / trust</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0749597825000172" target="_blank">The transparency dilemma: How AI disclosure erodes trust</a></td>
      <td>Oliver Schilke et al.</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Context dependent; transparency can be legally/ethically necessary even if trust penalty exists</td>
      <td>Nuanced source: disclosure can reduce trust, so transparency strategy must be handled carefully, not mechanically.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful</td>
      <td>AI disclosure / trust</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/02650487.2024.2401319" target="_blank">Effect of disclosing AI-generated content on prosocial advertising</a></td>
      <td>T. H. Baek et al.</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Prosocial advertising context; not directly SaaS</td>
      <td>Good example of how disclosure effects are context-sensitive and can influence attitudes/intentions.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful</td>
      <td>AI advertising effectiveness</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0148296324003710" target="_blank">Appeal types, self-efficacy and AI&#x27;s social role</a></td>
      <td>Y. Chen et al.</td>
      <td>2024</td>
      <td>Experimental; limited contexts</td>
      <td>Useful for choosing whether AI-created ads should use practical/agentic vs warm/communal appeals.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful</td>
      <td>AI advertising effectiveness</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698925001602" target="_blank">Generative AI advertisements and Human–AI collaboration</a></td>
      <td>J. C. Madathil et al.</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Specific to video ads and human-AI cues</td>
      <td>Useful for thinking about when human involvement should remain visible to protect perceived humanity and reduce anxiety.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI advertising effectiveness</td>
      <td><a href="https://www.columbia.edu/~on2110/Papers/AI_in_disguise.pdf" target="_blank">How AI-generated ads&#x27; visual cues shape consumer behavior</a></td>
      <td>Y. Exner et al.</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Specific dataset and ad context; quasi-experimental assumptions matter</td>
      <td>Useful because it links AI-generated visual cues to real ad behavior rather than opinions alone.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>Human-AI collaboration</td>
      <td><a href="https://arxiv.org/html/2503.18238v2" target="_blank">Collaborating with AI Agents: A Field Experiment on Ad Creation</a></td>
      <td>Research authors via arXiv</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Preprint; evaluate methods carefully</td>
      <td>Useful field evidence on human-AI collaboration in ad creation, productivity, and output quality.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>High</td>
      <td>AI productivity / workflows</td>
      <td><a href="https://arxiv.org/html/2510.12049v1" target="_blank">Generative AI and Firm Productivity: Field Experiments in Online Retail</a></td>
      <td>L. Fang et al.</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Preprint; online-retail setting; productivity != brand trust</td>
      <td>Shows GenAI value emerges from embedded workflows, not isolated prompts.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful/current</td>
      <td>B2B AI marketing practice</td>
      <td><a href="https://business.adobe.com/resources/reports/b2b-marketing-digital-trends.html" target="_blank">2025 AI and Digital Trends in B2B Journeys</a></td>
      <td>Adobe</td>
      <td>2025</td>
      <td>Vendor; promotes enterprise marketing software worldview</td>
      <td>Useful market signal for AI-driven B2B journeys, content effectiveness, and personalization.</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>Useful</td>
      <td>AI chatbots / conversational commerce</td>
      <td><a href="https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/cb.70130" target="_blank">Consumer Acceptance of Conversational Bots: Systematic Literature Review</a></td>
      <td>O. H. Fares et al.</td>
      <td>2025/2026</td>
      <td>Review article; customer-service/chatbot context varies</td>
      <td>Useful for designing AI chat interfaces that buyers can trust and use rather than resent.</td>
    </tr>
    </tbody>
</table>
</body>
</html>
```
